title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. romanica
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. romanica, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family, native to Romania. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. romanica include its ability to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
